options.name: --inputDirectory
options.description: to instrument (default: current directory)

options.name: --outputDirectory
options.description:
Optional, multiple: A folder to receive the
instrumented assemblies and their companions (
default: sub-folder '__Instrumented' of the
current directory; or '__Saved' if '--inplace'
is set).
See also '--inplace'

options.name: --symbolDirectory
options.description:
Optional, multiple: Additional directory to search
for matching symbols for the assemblies in the
input directory

options.name: --dependency
options.description:
Optional, multiple: assembly path to resolve
missing reference.

options.name: --key
options.description:
Optional, multiple: any other strong-name key to
use

options.name: --strongNameKey
options.description:
Optional: The default strong naming key to apply
to instrumented assemblies (default: None)

options.name: --report
options.description:
Optional: The output report template file (default:
'coverage.xml' or 'coverage.json' in the
current directory)

options.name: --fileFilter
options.description:
Optional, multiple: source file name to exclude
from instrumentation

options.name: --pathFilter
options.description:
Optional, multiple: source file path to exclude
from instrumentation

options.name: --assemblyFilter
options.description: instrumentation

options.name: --assemblyExcludeFilter
options.description:
Optional, multiple: assembly which links other
instrumented assemblies but for which internal
details may be excluded

options.name: --typeFilter
options.description:
Optional, multiple: type name to exclude from
instrumentation

options.name: --methodFilter
options.description:
Optional, multiple: method name to exclude from
instrumentation

options.name: --attributeFilter
options.description:
Optional, multiple: attribute name to exclude from
instrumentation

options.name: --attributetoplevel
options.description:
Optional, multiple: Types marked with an attribute
of a type that matches the regex are considered
top-level, and are not excluded from coverage on
the basis of any type which textually encloses
them.

options.name: --typetoplevel
options.description:
Optional, multiple: Types with a name that matches
the regex are considered top-level, and are not
excluded from coverage on the basis of any type
which textually encloses them.

options.name: --methodtoplevel
options.description:
matches the regex are considered top-level, and
are not excluded from coverage on the basis of
any method which textually encloses them.

options.name: --localSource
options.description:
Optional: Don't instrument code for which the
source file is not present.

options.name: --callContext
options.description:
Optional, multiple: Tracking either times of
visits in ticks or designated method calls
leading to the visits.
A single digit 0-7 gives the number of
decimal places of seconds to report; everything
else is at the mercy of the system clock
information available through DateTime.UtcNow
A string in brackets "[]" is interpreted as
an attribute type name (the trailing "Attribute"
is optional), so [Test] or [TestAttribute] will
match; if the name contains one or more ".",
then it will be matched against the full name of
the attribute type.
Other strings are interpreted as method
names (fully qualified if the string contains
any "." characters).

options.name: --reportFormat
options.description:
Optional: Generate the report in the specified
format (Json, NCover or the default OpenCover)

options.name: --inplace
options.description:
Optional: Instrument the inputDirectory, rather
than the outputDirectory (e.g. for dotnet test)

options.name: --save
options.description:
Optional: Write raw coverage data to file for
later processing

options.name: --zipfile
options.description:
Optional: Emit the coverage report inside a zip
archive.

options.name: --methodpoint
options.description:
Optional: record only whether a method has been
visited or not.  Overrides the --linecover and --
branchcover options.

options.name: --all
options.description:
Optional: record every hit at any location, and
for each context if --callContext is operating.

options.name: --linecover
options.description:
Optional: Do not record branch coverage.  Ignored
for the --reportFormat=NCover option.
Incompatible with --branchcover.

options.name: --branchcover
options.description:
Optional: Do not record line coverage.  Ignored
for the --reportFormat=NCover option.
Incompatible with --linecover.

options.name: --dropReturnCode
options.description:
Optional: Do not report any non-zero return code
from a launched process.

options.name: --sourcelink
options.description:
Optional: Display sourcelink URLs rather than file
paths if present.

options.name: --eager
options.description:
Optional, writes runner-mode coverage data
immediately.

options.name: --visibleBranches
options.description:
Optional: Hide complex internal IL branching
implementation details in switch/match
constructs, and just show what the source level
logic implies.

options.name: --showstatic
options.description:
Optional: Instrument and show code that is by
default skipped as trivial.  --showstatic:- is
equivalent to omitting the parameter; --
showstatic or --showstatic:+ sets the unvisited
count to a negative value interpreted by the
visualizer (but treated as zero by
ReportGenerator) ; --showstatic:++ sets the
unvisited count to zero.

options.name: --showGenerated
options.description:
Optional: Mark generated code with a visit count
of -2 (Automatic) for the Visualizer if unvisited

options.name: --trivia
options.description: Optional: Omit trivial sequence points

options.name: --portable
options.description:
Optional: portable operations - coverage report,
control file and recorded data co-located with
the recorder assembly

options.name: -q
options.description:
Optional, multiple: Quiet mode -- once to switch
off informational messages, twice to switch off
warnings as well, three (or more) times to
switch off all messages.

options.name: --verbose
options.description:
Optional, multiple: Verbose mode -- once to switch
on detailed messages (or undo one level of -q if
any have been set)

options.name: --help
options.description: Prints out the options.
